items.title: Malta Gaming Authority Breach: Wittmann Threatens to Release Full iGaming Archive
items.summary: German researcher Lilith Wittmann publicly claimed responsibility for breaching the MGA via a LinkedIn post, stating data had been shared with media and authorities. She warned that any extradition attempt would trigger immediate release of her entire iGaming data archive. The MGA — gatekeeper to 304 licensed operators generating €714M GVA in H1 2025 — has stayed largely silent on the substance of the allegations.

items.title: BOV Fraud Losses Nearly Double: €2.9M in 2024 to €5.4M in First 8 Months of 2025
items.summary: Bank of Valletta's Group Chief Anti-Financial Crime Officer revealed fraud losses nearly doubled from €2.9M in 2024 to €5.4M in just the first eight months of 2025. The Financial Crime Investigations Department was simultaneously probing €13M in scams reported in the first half of 2025 alone, with €3.5M attributed to Business Email Compromise attacks. BOV and the Malta Chamber launched a joint awareness programme in response.

items.title: Multi-Bank Phishing Scheme: 100+ Victims Across BOV, APS, HSBC and BNF Bank
items.summary: A sophisticated phishing operation used fake bank apps and SMS links mimicking BOV's login page to steal credentials from 100+ victims across four Maltese banks. Complaints began at BOV in late September 2024, spreading to APS Bank by May 2025, then HSBC and BNF. A 25-year-old suspect was arrested in February 2026 and denied bail, with investigations revealing possible international involvement and cryptocurrency money laundering.

items.title: BOV Prevented €3M in Fraud in 2025 as 3,700+ Scam Investigations Opened Since 2022
items.summary: BOV revealed its monitoring systems blocked €3M in fraud losses in 2025, though up to two-thirds of money lost to fraud is never recovered. Malta Police opened over 3,700 fraud investigations between 2022 and 2025. Police noted that fraudsters now use social media algorithms to identify victims and that up to €2M in defrauded funds was found in cryptocurrency last year alone, making recovery significantly harder.

items.title: Saint James Hospital Group Hit by Sophisticated Cyberattack
items.summary: Saint James Hospital Group confirmed a sophisticated cyberattack on 18 April 2025, immediately isolating all systems from external networks. External cybersecurity experts were engaged to conduct a forensic investigation into potential patient data exposure. The IDPC and police were formally notified — the first confirmed cyberattack on Maltese healthcare infrastructure.

items.title: Fast Track CRM Cyberattack Exposes Casino KYC Documents Including Passports
items.summary: Malta-headquartered iGaming CRM provider Fast Track was breached, exposing player KYC documents including passports and driver's licences across at least two casino operators. Shuffle Casino was the only publicly named victim and terminated its partnership with Fast Track. The breach occurred despite Fast Track holding a freshly renewed SOC 2 Type 2 certification from June 2025.

items.title: MTCA Email Error Exposes Tax Details of 7,000 Companies
items.summary: The Malta Tax and Customs Administration accidentally attached a file containing company names, registration numbers, tax IDs and contact details of approximately 7,000 companies to a mass email instead of using its secure portal. The IDPC was formally notified and an internal probe launched. The Opposition called for Finance Minister accountability and an independent investigation.

items.title: MSS Report: Cyberattacks Surged on Malta During UN Security Council and OSCE Tenures
items.summary: The Malta Security Service's annual reports for 2023 and 2024 — tabled in Parliament in 2025 — confirmed Malta experienced a surge in cyber incidents including phishing at public sector employees, ransomware targeting SMEs, and DDoS attacks during its UN Security Council membership and OSCE chairmanship. The MSS found no direct link to diplomatic activity but noted Malta's elevated geopolitical profile likely raised its cyber-risk level.
